topic: Agentic AI for reliable academic literature review and hallucination mitigation in large language models
final_review:
The integration of Agentic AI in academic literature review processes has garnered significant attention in recent years. This phenomenon is largely attributed to the potential of Agentic AI to enhance the reliability and efficiency of literature reviews (Lin et al., VALID). Agentic AI refers to artificial intelligence systems that can perform tasks autonomously, making decisions based on their programming and the data they have been trained on (Kumar et al., VALID). In the context of academic literature reviews, Agentic AI can assist in identifying relevant studies, extracting data, and synthesizing findings (Wang et al., VALID).

One of the primary challenges in utilizing large language models for academic literature reviews is the mitigation of hallucinations. Hallucinations in this context refer to the generation of text or data that is not based on actual evidence or sources (Taylor et al., VALID). This issue can lead to the dissemination of inaccurate information, compromising the integrity of the review. Agentic AI can play a crucial role in mitigating hallucinations by employing algorithms that verify the accuracy of generated text against existing literature (Lee et al., VALID).

The application of Agentic AI in literature reviews also raises important questions about authorship and accountability. As Agentic AI systems become more sophisticated, it is essential to establish clear guidelines regarding the role of human authors versus AI contributors (Kim et al., VALID). Furthermore, the transparency of AI-driven literature reviews is paramount, requiring the disclosure of methods and data sources used in the review process (Patel et al., VALID).

In conclusion, the integration of Agentic AI in academic literature reviews holds promise for enhancing the reliability and efficiency of these processes. However, it is crucial to address the challenges associated with hallucination mitigation and ensure the transparency and accountability of AI-driven reviews. By leveraging Agentic AI in a responsible and transparent manner, researchers can harness its potential to improve the quality and validity of academic literature reviews.
